rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT PICASO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Petition alleges a petition for order determining the identity of persons entitled to distribution will be required prior to final distribution. Unclear if notice of this petition is proper. 2) Petitioner is requesting for Modification of Prior Order Re Payment of Settlement Account in a Status Petition. Examiner requested legal authority in support of this request. No legal authority cited in the Memorandum of Points and Authorities filed. 3) Petition requests distribution be made in accordance with the percentage interest of each of the heirs at law Richard Holmes 50% = $200,000.00, James R. Picaso 10% = $40,000.00, Stephanie Picasco $40,000.00, Successor of Anne C. Whiteman 10% = $40,000.00, Virgina DeLaCruz-Klever 10% = $40,000.00, and Andrew J. Picasco 10% = $40,000.00. Examiner requested legal authority in support of this request. In the Memorandum of Points and Authorities attorney appears to amend/supplement the request without filing an amendment or supplement not verified by administrator and requests 40k each to James R. Picaso, Stephanie Picaso, Virginia DeLaCruz-Klever, and Andrew J. Picasco. "The distributive shares of Richard Holmes, 50% $200,000.000 and Ann C. Whiteman, 10% $40,000.00 be retained by the Administrator pending this Court's determination as to beneficiaries' entitlement to distribution of the estate"

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSE PAK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/2/22. (No earlier availability.) Examiner Notes (issues necessitating continuance): If the statutory fee/commission is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] Accounting was waived by both heirs, so no accounting has been filed and adjudicated. No courtesy copy of the declaration in support of the administrator's compensation for extraordinary services has been received by the assigned Examiner. No First Amended Partial No. 1 I&A was filed contemporaneously with this petition. Furthermore, the figures stated in paragraph 13 regarding the total value of the estate do not appear to be correct or consistent. Why are e-filing costs $11.50? They should be just $5.00 per e-filing. Brian D. Call filed a Request for Special Notice on 6/23/17. (The introduction refers to this as the Third Amended Petition) There is no need to use difficult-to-read footnotes: the petition should simply state when notice was published or on which date notice was given the FTB, etc., and footnotes are especially unnecessary when the date something was filed, such as an I&A, is already stated in the body of the petition. When a personal representative has been appointed by this Court, there is no need to further argue jurisdiction and venue. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/24/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CORINNE COTTRELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing estate set for 3/7/22. (The administrator was appointed and granted IAEA authority on 3/16/20; that order was not filed until 3/17/20.) CRC 2.110(a): Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. CRC 2.115: When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARTIN JOHN KEYS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/26/2022 1) In a Declaration by attorney, please file timesheets for the ordinary statutory services provided to the personal representative so that the Court may determine whether extraordinary fees are just and reasonable (Prob. C. 10811(a)). 2) Administrator must file detailed schedule of receipts. Schedule A showing 3 blocked groups of entries is not detailed. There must be an individual entry for each receipt. (CRC 7.550 (b)(6)). An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/10/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DANIEL MARK POMMON JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. Whether Probate Code 6124 applies to the Lost Will. 2) Issues in Janis Rachel Pommon Pozzi's Petition: a. In addition to all persons mentioned in the will, all heirs must be listed even if a decedent died testate. If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C. 1)]. 3) Issues In Jonathan Campbell's Petition: a. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.] b. Examiner does not have a proposed order. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN RICHARD STEVENS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The decedent's typewritten will was witnessed by two individuals. The subscribing witnesses did not sign the will under penalty of perjury. The petitioner has been unable to locate either subscribing witness. The petitioner alleges that he is familiar with the decedent's handwriting and signature. The petitioner also alleges that the decedent sent a copy of the will to his children including the petitioner. See Probate Code section 8221. See also section 8220(b) and CCP section 2015.5(a). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCIS JOSEPH LYNCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 6/11/97. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. [It is noted, however, that item 8 of the petition did not identify the trustee(s) of the devisee trust or explain whose child the niece is, as required by LRSF 14.18 C.2. and 3. Because a copy of the trust was attached and because the niece's last name is the same as one of the proposed co-executors', so she is presumably that petitioner's daughter, the oversight was waived.] Status of closing the estate will be set on 1/3/23. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 1/3/23, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PUI SUN LEUNG LEUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/26/2022 1) On page 2 #3.e.(3) Petitioner checked All hers at law are adults and have waived bond. Only Long Mei Leung Wong has filed a Waiver of Bond. Please explain. It appears Wilfred Kwong Pung Leung, Kwong Yau Liong, and Kong Mee Leung need to file waivers of bond. If bond is not waived by all heirs at law please provide the current market value of the real property so the court can determine proper bond. 2) Please explain why the DE-121 says Wilfred Kwong Ping Leung, Administrator when no administrator has been appointed in this case. Please re-notice without indicating Wilfred Kwong Ping Leong is administrator. (Probate Code 1202). 3) Please explain why in this estate the alleged 50% real property produces income but in the related matter #304966 it is alleged there is no annual gross income. 4) Please explain why in this estate the alleged 50% real property is valued at $250,000.00 when the related matter #304966 is alleged to be valued at $200,000.00. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/10/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/26/2022 1) On page 2 #3.e.(3) Petitioner checked All hers at law are adults and have waived bond. Only Long Mei Leung Wong has filed a Waiver of Bond. Please explain. It appears Wilfred Kwong Pung Leung, Kwong Yau Liong, and Kong Mee Leung need to file waivers of bond. If bond is not waived by all heirs at law please provide the current market value of the real property so the court can determine proper bond. 2) Please explain why the DE-121 says Wilfred Kwong Ping Leung, Administrator when no administrator has been appointed in this case. Please re-notice without indicating Wilfred Kwong Ping Leong is administrator. Please give notice to the Consulate General of Canada. (Probate Code 1202). 3) Please explain why in this estate the alleged 50% real property produces no income but in the related matter #304965 it is alleged there is annual gross income of $25,000.00. 4) Please explain why in this estate the alleged 50% real property is valued at $200,000.00 when the related matter #304965 is alleged to be valued at $275,000.00. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/10/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
